name: Deck
tagline: Spatial workspace for code — terminals, editors, browsers on one infinite canvas
story: Wanted my IDE, terminal, browser preview, and notes on one canvas instead of tabbed away into separate windows. Built it for Linux first because that's where I work.
description: Infinite-canvas workspace mixing real xterm + node-pty terminals, code-server / Monaco editors, webview browsers, and notes. Drag panels to dock into tab stacks, pop them out to their own OS window, group into labelled regions. Deep git integration (status, stage, commit, branches, diff, stash, worktrees), jump mode (tmux-style Tab + letter), token tracking for Claude Code / Codex / opencode, multi-canvas tabs, full persistence. GPU-composited drag at 60fps with dozens of panels.

name: TypinSpeed
tagline: Global typing test platform with real-time leaderboards
story: My friends and I spent too much time on MonkeyType. Building our own seemed like the obvious next step — and a better excuse to keep going.
description: Full-stack typing speed test with Firebase auth, real-time WPM tracking, and global leaderboard. Built to make typing practice competitive and addictive.

name: AI Flowchart
story: Spent too many hours on LucidChart making research diagrams look right. Decided the better investment was building something that does it for me.
description: Converts plain text descriptions into interactive flowcharts using LLMs. Built to make documentation visual and collaborative.

name: chess.coach
story: Wanted post-game analysis without uploading PGNs to a third party. Stockfish in the browser, an LLM that explains the why, and a board that scrubs through moves like a video.
description: Play vs Stockfish 18 (WASM, multi-threaded when crossOriginIsolated) right in the browser. Every move classified Best / Good / Inaccuracy / Mistake / Blunder via 12 internal detectors (hung piece, missed mate, allowed fork, early queen, lost tempo). Hint mode renders top-3 engine arrows. Eval bar with smooth transitions, captured material + point lead beside player tags. ← / → arrow keys scrub through played moves. Explore mode branches from any position — move both sides, see what would've happened, return to the live game. Time controls 1+0 → 30+0 with increment, Firebase Google sign-in + Firestore game history, optional NVIDIA NIM commentary via internal /api/coach edge proxy.

name: pdfforge
tagline: Linux-native PDF editor with lossless edits
story: Every PDF editor I tried flattened or re-rasterised on save. Wanted one that touches the file as little as the change demands — and ships native on Linux.
description: GTK4 + libadwaita PDF editor. Operates directly on PDF objects via pikepdf so page ops (rotate / reorder / split / merge) are byte-precise — no flatten round-trip. Highlight, underline, strikeout, ink, FreeText (bold/italic, inline editor), sticky notes, edit-text with block reflow. Per-tool style popover, annotations sidebar, search with active-match focus, atomic writes with pre-save validation, session restore.

name: API Tester
tagline: Local-first API client. Browser only — no backend, no login, no telemetry
story: Postman kept asking me to sign in to make a GET request. Built a client that runs entirely in the tab and forgets nothing because it never sent anything anywhere.
description: Browser-only API client with multi-tab requests, KV editors for params/headers, body modes (json/raw/urlencoded/multipart with multi-file drag), Bearer/Basic/API-Key auth, response viewer with collapsible JSON tree, JSON→TypeScript generator, JWT decode, environments with {{var}} interpolation and built-ins ($uuid, $timestamp, $now, $randomInt). cURL import + code-gen (cURL / fetch / axios / Python requests / Go net/http), visual assertion builder, 500-entry history persisted to localStorage.

name: Condense
tagline: Android share-sheet target that TL;DRs any article
story: Reading news on mobile means skimming intros and bouncing. Wanted a share-sheet button that hands me 3 sentences before I commit to the whole page.
description: Android app that summarises news / blog articles via Chrome's share sheet. Translucent ShareActivity pops a bottom sheet over the article, Cloud Function pulls HTML through Mozilla Readability and NVIDIA NIM (gpt-oss-120b), returns a 3–4 sentence TL;DR. Per-URL summary cache (shared across users) keeps repeat reads free, 50/day rate limit per user, Firestore for per-user entries, Firebase Auth via Credential Manager.

name: LLM → Notion
story: For the rare days I read something worth keeping — a paper, something technical. It captured the insight and pushed it to Notion before I could close the tab.
description: Desktop app that monitors clipboard, generates LLM summaries, and syncs to Notion. Built for seamless knowledge capture without breaking flow.

name: ML-Monitor
story: Everyone trains models. Almost nobody tracks when they start degrading. I wanted an alarm for when the black box quietly goes wrong.
description: Full-stack monitoring app that tracks ML model performance, detects data drift, and triggers alerts when accuracy drops >15% from baseline.
